raw_consumer_statement:
I am filing a complaint regarding American Expresss handling of a billing dispute related to a hotel reservation that was charged by XXXX without my clear consent. 

On XX/XX/XXXXXXXX XXXX XXXX I attempted to make an online reservation directly with XXXX XXXX in XXXX. Without any obvious notice, I was redirected to XXXX booking system. The webpage appeared to be the hotels own site, and I did not knowingly agree to book through XXXX. 

When I realized that the charge came from XXXX and not the hotel, I took a train and the next day arrived at the XXXX XXXX XXXX, approximatelty XXXX XXXX, contacted both XXXX and the hotel. I also filed a dispute with American Express, explaining that I had been misled into booking through a third-party site and that the merchant misrepresented itself in the transaction. 

American Express denied my dispute, stating that the merchant provided documentation validating the charge. However, Amex did not appear to review or address my explanation that the transaction was the result of a deceptive redirect and that I did not knowingly enter into an agreement with XXXX. 

I am requesting that American Express conduct a proper review of this dispute, including : Whether XXXX adequately disclosed that itnot the hotelwas the contracting merchant. 
Whether the booking terms and charges were clearly presented. 
Whether Amex complied with Regulation Z ( Truth in Lending Act ) in conducting a fair and reasonable dispute investigation. 

I am attaching supporting documentation, including correspondence with XXXX and screenshots of the booking page if available. 

I would appreciate CFPBs assistance in ensuring Amex reviews this dispute appropriately and considers the deceptive nature of the transaction.
